key_insight: PolyFusionAgent combines a multimodal polymer foundation model with a literature-grounded autonomous agent to predict properties and design new polymers in a single interactive workflow.
engineering_relevance: This framework can speed up polymer screening and inverse design for applications like energy storage and biomedicine by producing actionable, evidence-linked candidates rather than isolated predictions.
method: PolyFusion learns a shared latent space from multiple polymer representations: sequence, topology, 3D geometry, and fingerprints.
It is trained across millions of polymers to improve thermophysical property prediction and support property-conditioned generation.
PolyAgent retrieves polymer literature evidence to propose, evaluate, and contextualize design hypotheses in a closed loop.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports training across millions of polymers and improved property prediction, along with generation of chemically valid, structurally novel polymers beyond the reference design space.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of available polymer representations and literature evidence, which could limit generalization to underrepresented chemistries.
paper_title: PolyFusionAgent: A Multimodal Foundation Model and Autonomous AI Assistant for Polymer Property Prediction and Inverse Design
